 {"id":"gt-6m3e","title":"bd create --dedup: semantic deduplication before issue creation","description":"Before creating issue, check for semantic duplicates using AI similarity.

**From VC**: internal/deduplication/ - AI-powered batch comparison. ~300 lines.
VC had issue pollution problem: 438 issues with ~350+ spam because no early dedup.

**Gas Town implementation**: CLI flag on bd create:
```bash
bd create --dedup --title="Fix auth bug" --description="..."
```

Checks recent issues (7-day window) with AI similarity. If confidence >0.85, warns or blocks.

**Value**: Prevents pollution from parallel workers discovering same issues.

**VC lesson**: 115 issues filed in single day (Nov 2) because supervisor over-discovered without dedup. Rate limiting + dedup are essential.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-20T20:30:17.305652-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T20:30:17.305652-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-6m3e","depends_on_id":"gt-zhpa","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-20T20:30:27.599806-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-6n13","title":"Competing molecule instantiation mechanisms need resolution","description":"Documentation describes 4 different molecule instantiation methods:
1. bd mol bond (beads-based, in molecules.md)
2. gt sling (proposed in sling-design.md)
3. gt swarm (implemented in code)
4. gt molecule instantiate (in architecture.md)

Role prompts use different methods:
- Deacon: bd mol spawn
- Refinery: gt mol bond
- Polecat: bd mol current

Need to:
1. Decide canonical mechanism
2. Update all role prompts to use it
3. Remove/deprecate alternatives
4. Update architecture docs","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-24T12:50:56.093813-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-24T23:48:51.018169-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-24T23:48:51.018169-08:00","close_reason":"Superseded by gt-mbyy: comprehensive CLI API audit that covers molecule mechanisms plus full command structure review","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-6n13","depends_on_id":"gt-jo9n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-24T12:52:05.105811-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-9pyg","title":"Deacon tmux status bar: heartbeat timing and patrol state","description":"## Problem

The deacon shows patrol output but lacks real-time observability:
- No indication of when last heartbeat occurred
- No indication of when next heartbeat is scheduled
- No visibility into current patrol step

## Desired Behavior

The tmux status bar for gt-deacon should show:

```
⛪ Deacon | ❤️ 2m ago | ⏰ 3m | 📍 health-scan (3/7)
```

Components:
- **Role icon**: ⛪ (deacon identity)
- **Last heartbeat**: ❤️ 2m ago (time since last heartbeat file update)
- **Next heartbeat**: ⏰ 3m (time until daemon would poke)
- **Current step**: 📍 health-scan (3/7) (current patrol atom, step N of M)

## Implementation

1. **Read heartbeat.json** for last update time
2. **Calculate next poke** based on daemon interval (default 5m)
3. **Read current wisp** from .beads-wisp/ to get patrol progress
4. **Update tmux status** periodically or on state change

Options:
- tmux status-right with shell script
- gt deacon status --tmux for formatted output
- Hook into patrol step completion

## Related

- gt-id36: Deacon Kernel epic
- gt-3x0z: Wisp Molecule Integration","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-22T03:03:38.693983-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T03:03:38.693983-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-hgk","title":"Mail system: message types and threading","description":"GGT mail system needs message types and threading like PGT.

## 1. Message Types
Add to internal/mail/types.go:
```go
type MessageType string
const (
    TypeTask         MessageType = "task"         // Required processing
    TypeScavenge     MessageType = "scavenge"     // Optional first-come work
    TypeNotification MessageType = "notification" // Informational
    TypeReply        MessageType = "reply"        // Response to message
)

// Update Message struct
type Message struct {
    // existing fields...
    Type     MessageType `json:"type"`
    ThreadID string      `json:"thread_id,omitempty"`
    ReplyTo  string      `json:"reply_to,omitempty"`
}
```

## 2. Priority Levels
Expand from 2 to 4:
```go
type Priority string
const (
    PriorityLow    Priority = "low"
    PriorityNormal Priority = "normal"
    PriorityHigh   Priority = "high"
    PriorityUrgent Priority = "urgent"
)
```

## 3. CLI Updates
internal/cmd/mail.go:
- Add --type flag to send: `gt mail send ... --type task`
- Add --reply-to flag: `gt mail send ... --reply-to <msg-id>`
- Add thread command: `gt mail thread <thread-id>`

## 4. Threading Logic
NewMessage() should auto-generate thread_id if not a reply.
Reply messages inherit thread_id from original.

## Files to Modify
- internal/mail/types.go: Add types, expand Priority
- internal/mail/mailbox.go: Thread filtering
- internal/cmd/mail.go: CLI flags and thread command

## PGT Reference
gastown-py/src/gastown/mail/message.py

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Messages have type field (default: notification)
- [ ] 4 priority levels supported
- [ ] Reply creates thread with shared thread_id
- [ ] gt mail thread <id> shows conversation","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T14:46:55.29463-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T20:14:28.308997-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-18T20:14:28.308997-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-hj9e","title":"Remove pinned field workaround after beads fix","description":"## Summary

Once bd-phtv is fixed in beads, we can remove the workaround documentation and potentially simplify the pinToHook() implementation.

## Current Workaround

The handoff bead attachment mechanism (AttachMolecule) is used as the primary work assignment mechanism instead of the pinned field. This works correctly but adds complexity.

## Changes After Beads Fix

1. Remove the NOTE comment in internal/beads/beads.go:Pin() explaining the bug
2. Consider simplifying pinToHook() if the pinned field becomes reliable
3. Update gt-o3is to reference the fix

## Blocked By

This issue is blocked by external:beads:pinned-field-fix (bd-phtv).
When that capability is shipped, this issue becomes ready.

## Related

- gt-o3is: Original investigation that found this bug
- bd-phtv: The beads fix (in ~/gt/beads)","notes":"BLOCKED: Can't add external:beads:pinned-field-fix dependency due to bd-ucgz (migration invariants bug). Once that's fixed, add: bd dep add gt-hj9e external:beads:pinned-field-fix","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T12:35:32.316302-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T12:37:18.526987-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-l3c","title":"Design: Polecat Beads write access","description":"Design for granting polecats direct beads write access.

## Background

With Beads v0.30.0 tombstone-based rearchitecture, we have solid multi-agent support. Reversing the original read-only decision.

## Benefits

- Simplifies architecture (no mail-based issue filing proxy)
- Empowers polecats to file discovered work
- Beads handles work-disavowal

## Complications

For OSS projects where you cannot commit to project .beads/, need per-rig beads repo configuration.

## Subtasks (implementation)

- gt-zx3: Per-rig beads configuration schema
- gt-e1y: Worker prompting updates for beads access
- gt-cjb: Witness proxy removal
- gt-082: Beads sync in decommission checklist

**Design complete.** Each subtask has full specification in its description.","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-15T19:37:42.191734-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T01:30:41.67682-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-25T01:30:41.67682-08:00","deleted_by":"batch delete","delete_reason":"batch delete","original_type":"epic"}
 {"id":"gt-l4gm","title":"Crew workers: design documentation and remaining work","description":"## Summary

Crew workers are user-managed persistent workspaces within a rig, distinct from polecats.

## Current Implementation

### Commands (all working)
- `gt crew add <name>` - Create workspace (git clone)
- `gt crew list` - List workspaces with status
- `gt crew at <name>` - Attach to tmux session
- `gt crew remove <name>` - Remove workspace
- `gt crew pristine [<name>]` - git pull + bd sync
- `gt crew refresh <name>` - Context cycle with handoff mail
- `gt crew status [<name>]` - Detailed status
- `gt crew rename <name>` - Rename workspace

### Crew vs Polecat

| Aspect | Crew | Polecat |
|--------|------|---------|
| Lifecycle | User-managed | Witness-managed |
| Scheduling | Manual | `gt spawn` |
| Merge flow | Direct push OK | Integration branch → refinery |
| Garbage collection | Never | Auto on completion |
| Identity | Long-lived (emma, dave) | Ephemeral (Nux, Toast) |

## Known Bugs

- **gt-70b3**: detectSender() doesn't recognize crew workers
- **gt-vdp0**: Crew CLAUDE.md shows Refinery template

## Potential Enhancements

1. Rebase helper in `gt crew pristine` for conflict resolution
2. Cross-rig crew support (crew worker in multiple rigs?)
3. Better mail identity auto-detection for crew","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-18T21:49:58.524424-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T21:49:58.524424-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-mx6s","title":"Witness patrol wisp with polecat leases","description":"Witness should maintain a rolling patrol wisp that tracks active polecats:

```
wisp-witness-patrol
├── lease: furiosa (boot → working → done)
├── lease: nux (working)
└── lease: slit (done, closed)
```

When POLECAT_STARTED arrives:
- bd mol bond mol-polecat-lease wisp-patrol --var polecat=X

Patrol loop iterates leases:
- gt peek $polecat
- If idle: gt nudge
- If shutdown received: close lease

When all leases closed:
- bd mol squash wisp-xxx --summary='N polecats processed'

Requires mol-polecat-lease proto definition.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-22T22:01:13.640901-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T04:39:28.525208-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T04:39:28.525208-08:00","close_reason":"Superseded by Christmas Ornament pattern (gt-tnow, gt-qflq).  {"id":"gt-qp98","title":"Refactor: Eliminate state.json, use beads assignee field for polecat state","description":"## Problem

state.json in each polecat worktree duplicates data that should be in beads:
- `issue` field duplicates assignment (should be issue.assignee)
- `state` field duplicates status (derivable from issue.status)
- `name/rig/branch/clone_path` are all derivable from filesystem/git

This violates 'Beads as the data plane' (HOP Decision 001).

## Current State

```json
{
  "name": "Angharad",        // Derivable: basename(cwd)
  "rig": "gastown",          // Derivable: parent dir name
  "state": "working",        // Should be: issue.status
  "clone_path": "/path",     // Derivable: cwd
  "branch": "polecat/X",     // Derivable: git branch
  "issue": "gt-xyz",         // Should be: issue.assignee
  "timestamps": "..."        // Should be in beads history
}
```

## Target State

**Polecat identity**: Derived from worktree path
**Polecat assignment**: `issue.assignee = 'gastown/Angharad'`
**Polecat state**: Derived from issue.status (in_progress = working, closed = done)
**Polecat existence**: Worktree directory exists

## Benefits

1. Single source of truth (no sync issues)
2. Queryable: `bd list --assignee=gastown/Angharad`
3. History tracking via beads
4. Cross-agent coordination via beads sync
5. Simpler code (no state file management)

## Implementation

1. Update `gt spawn` to set issue.assignee instead of writing state.json
2. Update polecat list/status commands to query beads
3. Update Witness to query beads for polecat state
4. Remove state.json read/write code
5. Keep state.json as optional bootstrap cache (perf optimization only)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T11:40:33.389689-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T12:07:43.519059-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T12:07:43.519059-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-tca","title":"Polecats should auto-cleanup after MR submission","description":"Currently polecats must manually run 'gt handoff --shutdown' after completing work. This is error-prone and leaves stale polecats around.

## Desired Flow

1. Polecat completes work
2. Polecat runs 'gt mq submit' (or similar)
3. MR is added to integration queue
4. **Polecat automatically cleans up** (no manual handoff needed)

## Implementation Options

### Option A: mq submit triggers cleanup
In 'gt mq submit':
1. Submit MR to queue
2. Automatically run cleanup (same as gt handoff --shutdown)
3. Polecat session terminates

### Option B: Refinery triggers cleanup
When Refinery picks up MR:
1. Refinery processes MR
2. Sends message to Witness: 'CLEANUP: <polecat>'
3. Witness cleans up polecat

### Option C: Molecule-driven
Define cleanup as final phase of polecat-work molecule:
1. code → test → submit-mr → cleanup

## Note
This reinforces the ephemeral model: polecats exist only for the duration of a single task.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-20T15:22:54.485456-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T11:44:14.020856-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T11:44:14.020856-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented: mq submit now auto-cleanups polecats after MR submission","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-tca","depends_on_id":"gt-9nf","type":"related","created_at":"2025-12-20T15:40:08.998908-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-th7","title":"Add agent abstraction layer to support non-Claude agents","description":"Currently Gas Town hardcodes 'claude --dangerously-skip-permissions' throughout the codebase for spawning agents. We should add an abstraction layer to support other AI agents (e.g., Gemini CLI, OpenAI agents, local models).

Locations that spawn Claude:
- internal/cmd/mayor.go:131
- internal/cmd/deacon.go:150  
- internal/cmd/witness.go:280
- internal/cmd/crew.go (multiple locations)
- internal/cmd/up.go:190, 229
- internal/session/manager.go:146
- internal/refinery/manager.go:207

Suggested approach:
1. Create an agent package with an interface
2. Add configuration for agent type in town/rig config
3. Replace hardcoded claude commands with agent.Spawn() calls
4. Support agents: claude, gemini, openai, local (ollama, etc.)","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-20T15:11:15.931048-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T15:26:54.236995-08:00"}
